_meta.license: Synthetic educational reference content for qualified review. No warranty.
_meta.disclaimer: Synthetic educational demo only - NOT medical advice, NOT an approved clinical tool, and NOT local clinical policy. Do not enter, transmit, or infer PHI or real encounter details. Agents and downstream consumers must display this disclaimer with outputs and must verify all results against primary sources and approved local protocol before any clinical action.

data.fullName: Intravenous Tenecteplase Compared with Alteplase for Acute Ischaemic Stroke in Canada
data.applicabilityNotes: Pragmatic, non-inferior — supports TNK as first-line in eligible AIS, especially when EVT is anticipated.
data.limitations: Open-label; primary endpoint included mRS 0-1 only.
data.practiceImpact: Supports TNK 0.25 mg/kg as a preferred IV thrombolytic for IVT-eligible AIS within 4.5 h.

data.fullName: Trial of Tenecteplase in Chinese Patients with Acute Ischemic Stroke
data.applicabilityNotes: External validity in East Asian population.
data.limitations: Excluded LVO with intended EVT in many sites.
data.practiceImpact: Confirms non-inferiority of TNK in non-LVO eligible AIS within 4.5 h.

data.fullName: Tenecteplase vs Alteplase in AIS (ORIGINAL)
data.practiceImpact: Adds further RCT support for TNK as alteplase-equivalent first-line.

data.fullName: MRI-Guided Thrombolysis for Stroke with Unknown Time of Onset

secondaryEndpoints.name: Mortality at 90 d
secondaryEndpoints.result: Numerically higher with alteplase, not significant

data.applicabilityNotes: Applies to MRI-equipped centers; supports MRI-based selection in unknown-onset stroke.
data.limitations: Early termination; modest absolute benefit with non-trivial sICH increase.
data.practiceImpact: Foundational evidence for imaging-selected thrombolysis in wake-up / unknown onset stroke.

data.fullName: Thrombolysis Guided by Perfusion Imaging up to 9 Hours after Onset

keyInclusion: Perfusion-imaging mismatch (core <70 mL, mismatch ratio >1.2, mismatch volume >10 mL)

data.applicabilityNotes: Population had ~70% non-LVO; provides direct evidence for late-window selection in EVT-ineligible.
data.limitations: Stopped early after WAKE-UP results; modest sample size.
data.practiceImpact: Supports perfusion-mismatch IVT in 4.5-9 h window when EVT not indicated.

data.fullName: Echoplanar Imaging Thrombolytic Evaluation Trial

primaryEndpoint.result: Reduced infarct growth in mismatch group; clinical co-primary not significant

data.applicabilityNotes: Hypothesis-generating for mismatch-based selection.
data.limitations: Small; surrogate endpoint primary.
data.practiceImpact: Conceptual basis for later mismatch-based late-window trials.

data.fullName: Extending the Time Window for IV Thrombolysis Using MRI-based Selection

primaryEndpoint.result: Favored alteplase numerically; not statistically significant after early termination

data.applicabilityNotes: Trial stopped early after WAKE-UP / EXTEND results.
data.limitations: Underpowered after early stop.
data.practiceImpact: Adds to the late-window mismatch evidence base; not independently practice-changing.

data.fullName: Tenecteplase for Stroke at 4.5 to 24 Hours with Perfusion-Imaging Selection

primaryEndpoint.result: Favored TNK numerically; primary endpoint did not reach significance overall

data.applicabilityNotes: Population had high EVT use; benefit may have been masked.
data.limitations: Statistically non-significant primary; subgroup signals only.
data.practiceImpact: Cautionary: late-window TNK alone (with EVT permitted) did not meet its primary endpoint.

data.fullName: Tenecteplase in Wake-up Ischaemic Stroke Trial
data.applicabilityNotes: Negative for unselected wake-up TNK; reinforces need for imaging-based selection.
data.limitations: Open-label; no advanced imaging.
data.practiceImpact: Late-window thrombolysis without advanced imaging is not supported.

data.fullName: Tenecteplase for Ischemic Stroke 4.5-24 Hours without Thrombectomy
data.applicabilityNotes: Pure EVT-ineligible population — directly supports the SISTER niche.
data.limitations: Single-region (China); needs replication.
data.practiceImpact: Strongest evidence to date for late-window TNK in EVT-ineligible LVO.

data.fullName: Trial of Endovascular Thrombectomy for Large Ischemic Strokes

safetyFindings.other: Vascular complications more frequent with EVT

data.imagingCriteria: CT or MRI; ASPECTS 3-5 or core 50-100 mL
data.applicabilityNotes: Establishes EVT benefit for large-core anterior-circulation LVO up to 24 h.
data.limitations: Open-label; modest absolute benefit at lower ASPECTS.
data.practiceImpact: Supports EVT for large-core; large core is no longer an automatic exclusion.

data.fullName: Endovascular Therapy for Acute Ischemic Stroke with Large Infarct
data.applicabilityNotes: Chinese population; complementary to SELECT2.
data.limitations: Open-label.
data.practiceImpact: Confirms EVT benefit in large-core population.

data.fullName: Endovascular Therapy for Acute Stroke with Large Ischemic Region
data.applicabilityNotes: Established large-core EVT benefit pre-SELECT2 / ANGEL-ASPECT.
data.limitations: Single-country.
data.practiceImpact: First major RCT to support large-core EVT.

data.fullName: Endovascular Thrombectomy for AIS with Established Large Infarct
data.limitations: Stopped early for efficacy.
data.practiceImpact: European replication of large-core EVT benefit.

data.fullName: Endovascular Treatment of Acute Basilar-Artery Occlusion
data.applicabilityNotes: Chinese population; ≤12 h window.
data.limitations: Open-label; conducted in China; stopped early.
data.practiceImpact: Established EVT benefit for basilar-artery occlusion within 12 h.

data.fullName: Thrombectomy 6 to 24 Hours after Stroke Due to Basilar-Artery Occlusion
data.imagingCriteria: Basilar-artery occlusion, 6-24 h from estimated onset
data.applicabilityNotes: Extends basilar EVT to the 6-24 h window.
data.limitations: Open-label; conducted in China; stopped early for efficacy.
data.practiceImpact: Extends EVT benefit for basilar-artery occlusion to 6-24 h.

data.fullName: Thrombectomy 6 to 24 Hours after Stroke with Mismatch between Deficit and Infarct
data.applicabilityNotes: Established late-window EVT.
data.practiceImpact: Foundational evidence for late-window EVT.

data.fullName: Thrombectomy at 6-16 Hours with Selection by Perfusion Imaging

keyInclusion: Core <70 mL, mismatch ratio >1.8, mismatch volume >15 mL

data.practiceImpact: Established perfusion-mismatch criteria for late-window EVT.

data.fullName: Intra-arterial Alteplase Following Successful Thrombectomy
data.applicabilityNotes: Hypothesis-generating; replication ongoing.
data.limitations: Small; stopped early.
data.practiceImpact: IA alteplase after successful EVT may improve outcomes; not yet standard of care.

data.fullName: Thrombolysis for Central Retinal Artery Occlusion
data.applicabilityNotes: CRAO thrombolysis remains investigational; benefit population-uncertain.
data.limitations: Small; underpowered.
data.practiceImpact: CRAO IVT is reasonable in selected centers; no strong RCT support yet.
data.verificationNotes: Numeric primary-endpoint precision left qualitative pending manual verification.

data.fullName: Care Bundle Approach for Acute Intracerebral Haemorrhage
data.intervention: Care bundle (intensive BP lowering to <140 mmHg, glucose 6.1-7.8 mmol/L, temperature <37.5°C, anticoagulant reversal)
data.applicabilityNotes: Implementation-focused: bundle not single-component.
data.limitations: Cluster-randomized; bundle effects cannot be attributed to single component.
data.practiceImpact: Establishes care-bundle approach for acute ICH.

data.fullName: Andexanet for Factor Xa Inhibitor-Associated Acute ICH

population.timeWindow: ≤15 h from last FXa dose, ≤6 h from ICH onset

data.applicabilityNotes: Hemostatic benefit comes with thrombotic-risk trade-off.
data.limitations: Open-label; usual care heterogeneous.
data.practiceImpact: AHA/ASA 2022 lists andexanet as Class IIa for FXa-associated ICH; ANNEXA-I refines hemostatic-vs-thrombotic trade-off.

data.fullName: Early Minimally Invasive Removal of Intracerebral Hemorrhage

keyInclusion: ICH 30-80 mL, lobar (after early stop in deep cohort)

data.intervention: Early minimally invasive parafascicular evacuation + standard care
data.applicabilityNotes: Adaptive design enriched for lobar; deep ICH did not benefit and was stopped.
data.limitations: Open-label; specific device.
data.practiceImpact: Supports early minimally invasive evacuation for lobar ICH at capable centers.

data.fullName: Clopidogrel with Aspirin in Acute Minor Stroke or TIA
data.intervention: Aspirin + clopidogrel x 21 days, then clopidogrel x 90 d
data.applicabilityNotes: Chinese population; ABCD² ≥4 or NIHSS ≤3.
data.practiceImpact: Founding evidence for short-course DAPT in minor stroke / high-risk TIA.

data.fullName: Clopidogrel and Aspirin in Acute Ischemic Stroke and High-Risk TIA
data.applicabilityNotes: Bleeding excess emerges after ~21 days, supporting short-course strategy.
data.practiceImpact: Reinforces 21-day cap on DAPT for minor stroke.

data.fullName: Ticagrelor and Aspirin or Aspirin Alone in AIS or TIA
data.applicabilityNotes: Ticagrelor-based alternative to clopidogrel-based DAPT.
data.practiceImpact: Supports ticagrelor-based DAPT, especially when CYP2C19 LOF suspected.

data.fullName: Dual Antiplatelet Treatment up to 72 Hours after Ischemic Stroke

keyInclusion: Mild AIS or high-risk TIA, ≤72 h

data.intervention: Aspirin + clopidogrel x 21 days, started up to 72 h
data.applicabilityNotes: Extends DAPT eligibility window from 24 h to 72 h.
data.practiceImpact: Allows DAPT initiation up to 72 h in eligible mild stroke.

data.fullName: Ticagrelor or Clopidogrel with Aspirin in High-Risk TIA or Minor Stroke
data.applicabilityNotes: Pharmacogenomic-guided antiplatelet strategy.
data.limitations: Genotype testing not always available.
data.practiceImpact: Supports ticagrelor over clopidogrel in CYP2C19 LOF carriers.

data.fullName: Early versus Later Anticoagulation for Stroke with Atrial Fibrillation
data.intervention: Early DOAC (within 48 h for minor, 6-7 d for major)
data.comparator: Delayed DOAC (>3-4 d for minor, 12-14 d for major)

primaryEndpoint.definition: Composite of recurrent stroke / SE / major bleeding at 30 d

data.applicabilityNotes: Used stroke severity (CT-derived) to calibrate timing.
data.limitations: Confidence interval crosses 1 for primary; secondary supports early.
data.practiceImpact: Supports early DOAC initiation per stroke severity.

data.fullName: Timing of Oral Anticoagulant Therapy in AIS with AF

primaryEndpoint.definition: Composite ischemic event / sICH / mortality at 90 d

data.applicabilityNotes: Smaller than ELAN; consistent direction.
data.limitations: Underpowered for superiority.
data.practiceImpact: Supports earlier DOAC initiation.

data.fullName: Apixaban in Patients with Atrial Fibrillation Unsuitable for VKA

keyInclusion: AF with ≥1 stroke risk factor; not VKA candidate

data.applicabilityNotes: AF stroke prevention superiority of apixaban over aspirin in VKA-unsuitable patients. Did NOT enrol ICH survivors — background context, not direct ICH-survivor evidence.
data.limitations: Pre-DOAC adoption era; comparator is aspirin; prior-ICH patients not enrolled.
data.practiceImpact: Supports anticoagulation over aspirin for AF stroke prevention generally; the ICH-survivor question is answered by the dedicated trials below.

data.fullName: Apixaban for Stroke Prevention in Subclinical Atrial Fibrillation

keyInclusion: Subclinical AF on cardiac implantable device

data.applicabilityNotes: Subclinical-AF trial illustrating the stroke-vs-bleeding trade-off; it did NOT enrol ICH survivors, so it is background rather than direct evidence for that population.
data.limitations: Subclinical AF only; prior-ICH patients not enrolled.
data.practiceImpact: Establishes apixaban benefit in subclinical AF; the ICH-survivor question is addressed by PRESTIGE-AF, SoSTART, APACHE-AF and the COCROACH meta-analysis, which enrolled that population.

data.fullName: Intensive BP Control after Endovascular Thrombectomy
data.applicabilityNotes: Post-EVT BP intensive lowering harmful; conventional control preferred.
data.limitations: Single-region trial (China).
data.practiceImpact: Avoid intensive BP lowering after successful EVT; target 140-180 mmHg.

data.fullName: Optimal Timing of Anticoagulation after Acute Ischaemic Stroke with Atrial Fibrillation

primaryEndpoint.definition: Composite: recurrent ischaemic stroke, sICH, unclassifiable stroke, systemic embolism

data.applicabilityNotes: Enrolled across infarct sizes; supports not delaying initiation.
data.limitations: Open-label timing; blinded endpoint.
data.practiceImpact: Early DOAC (≤4 d) non-inferior to delayed — do not routinely delay anticoagulation.

data.fullName: IPD Meta-analysis of Anticoagulation Timing after Ischaemic Stroke with AF

primaryEndpoint.definition: Composite: recurrent ischaemic stroke, sICH, unclassified stroke

data.applicabilityNotes: Individual-patient-data pooling of 4 RCTs.
data.limitations: Meta-analysis of open-label-timing trials.
data.practiceImpact: Pooled IPD shows early DOAC (≤4 d) reduces 30-d composite — supports early initiation.

data.fullName: Rivaroxaban for Stroke Prevention after Embolic Stroke of Undetermined Source
data.limitations: Stopped early for futility/harm.
data.practiceImpact: Rivaroxaban not superior to aspirin in ESUS and more bleeding — empiric anticoagulation not indicated.

data.fullName: Dabigatran for Prevention of Stroke after Embolic Stroke of Undetermined Source
data.practiceImpact: Dabigatran not superior to aspirin in ESUS — confirms NAVIGATE; no empiric DOAC.

data.fullName: Apixaban to Prevent Recurrence after Cryptogenic Stroke in Patients with Atrial Cardiopathy
data.applicabilityNotes: Atrial cardiopathy: P-wave terminal force, NT-proBNP, or LA diameter.
data.limitations: Stopped for futility.
data.practiceImpact: Apixaban no better than aspirin in atrial-cardiopathy ESUS — atrial cardiopathy alone does not justify anticoagulation.

data.fullName: Thrombectomy for Stroke with a Large Infarct of Unrestricted Size
data.imagingCriteria: ASPECTS ≤5, no upper infarct-size limit, ≤6.5 h
data.applicabilityNotes: Extends EVT to very large infarcts.
data.limitations: Stopped early; more sICH.
data.practiceImpact: Thrombectomy benefits even very large infarcts (ASPECTS ≤5), at the cost of more sICH.

data.fullName: Long-Term Outcomes of Patent Foramen Ovale Closure or Medical Therapy after Stroke
data.applicabilityNotes: Long-term follow-up of the RESPECT cohort.
data.practiceImpact: Long-term PFO closure reduces recurrent stroke vs medical therapy in selected patients <60 y.

data.fullName: Patent Foramen Ovale Closure or Anticoagulation vs Antiplatelets after Stroke

keyInclusion: PFO with atrial septal aneurysm or large shunt

data.imagingCriteria: Atrial septal aneurysm or large shunt
data.applicabilityNotes: High-risk PFO anatomy.
data.practiceImpact: Closure markedly cuts recurrent stroke in high-risk PFO anatomy (ASA / large shunt).

data.fullName: Patent Foramen Ovale Closure or Antiplatelet Therapy for Cryptogenic Stroke
data.practiceImpact: Closure reduces recurrent stroke and new infarcts; watch for AF and device events.

data.fullName: Rapid Blood-Pressure Lowering in Acute Intracerebral Hemorrhage
data.limitations: Dichotomous primary not met; ordinal analysis favored intensive lowering.
data.practiceImpact: Supports early BP reduction in ICH; ordinal benefit despite a negative dichotomous primary.

data.fullName: Intensive Blood-Pressure Lowering in Acute Cerebral Hemorrhage

safetyFindings.other: More renal AEs with intensive target

data.applicabilityNotes: Argues against targeting <140 vs ~140.
data.limitations: Stopped for futility.
data.practiceImpact: More intensive target (<140) gave no benefit and more renal AEs — avoid overshoot below ~140.

data.fullName: Minimally Invasive Surgery with Thrombolysis in ICH Evacuation

safetyFindings.other: Outcome better when residual clot ≤15 mL

data.applicabilityNotes: Functional outcome tied to extent of clot evacuation.
data.limitations: Negative primary; benefit signal only with adequate evacuation.
data.practiceImpact: No overall functional benefit; safe; outcome depends on achieving adequate evacuation.

data.fullName: Stenting versus Endarterectomy for Carotid-Artery Stenosis

primaryEndpoint.definition: Composite periprocedural stroke/MI/death or 4-y ipsilateral stroke

safetyFindings.other: Stroke favors CEA; MI favors CAS

data.applicabilityNotes: Age interaction: CAS worse periprocedural stroke in older patients.
data.practiceImpact: CAS and CEA equivalent on the composite; stroke risk favors CEA, MI risk favors CAS.

data.fullName: Asundexian for Secondary Stroke Prevention

keyInclusion: Noncardioembolic ischaemic stroke or high-risk TIA

data.intervention: Asundexian 50 mg once daily + antiplatelet therapy
data.applicabilityNotes: First positive phase-3 factor XIa inhibitor for secondary prevention.
data.limitations: On-top-of-antiplatelet design; long-term safety still accruing.
data.practiceImpact: Asundexian on top of antiplatelets cut ischaemic stroke ~26% without more major bleeding — could reshape noncardioembolic prevention.

data.fullName: Three Low-Dose Antihypertensive Agents in a Single Pill after Intracerebral Hemorrhage
data.intervention: Single-pill triple therapy: telmisartan 20 mg / amlodipine 2.5 mg / indapamide 1.25 mg + standard care

safetyFindings.other: Early discontinuation 13.6% vs 6.0%, mainly ≥20% serum creatinine rise

data.applicabilityNotes: Simplified low-dose triple pill after ICH; 2-week active run-in before randomisation.
data.limitations: Higher adverse-event discontinuation with triple pill.
data.practiceImpact: A simplified low-dose triple pill after ICH lowers recurrent stroke and CV events — supports intensive, simplified BP-lowering.

data.fullName: Endovascular Thrombectomy for Large-Core Ischaemic Stroke up to 24 h — Systematic Review and IPD Meta-analysis with Central Imaging Adjudication

keyInclusion: ASPECTS ≤5 or estimated ischaemic core ≥50 mL

data.imagingCriteria: ASPECTS ≤5 or core ≥50 mL; central imaging core-lab readjudication
data.applicabilityNotes: Benefit sustained across ASPECTS / core strata to 24 h; uncertain for core ≥150 mL beyond 6 h.
data.limitations: Wide CIs for very extensive cores (≥150 mL); trial-level heterogeneity.
data.practiceImpact: Consolidates EVT benefit across large-core strata to 24 h, except very extensive cores (≥150 mL) beyond 6 h where evidence remains limited.

data.fullName: Ticagrelor with Aspirin Dual Antiplatelet Therapy combined with Intravenous Thrombolysis in Patients with Ischaemic Stroke in China
data.intervention: Oral aspirin + ticagrelor DAPT within 6 h (ticagrelor days 2-7)

safetyFindings.other: Wide CIs preclude excluding a small sICH increase

data.applicabilityNotes: Early oral DAPT as an adjunct to thrombolysis in moderate stroke; single-country (China) trial.
data.limitations: Open-label aspirin days 2-90; sICH CIs wide.
data.practiceImpact: Early oral DAPT within 6 h as an adjunct to thrombolysis improved excellent outcomes in moderate stroke — a strategy long considered contraindicated; small sICH risk not excluded.

data.fullName: Adjunctive Intra-Arterial Alteplase After Successful Thrombectomy for Acute Ischemic Stroke
data.intervention: Thrombectomy + intra-arterial alteplase 0.225 mg/kg (max 20 mg) over 15 min
data.applicabilityNotes: Open-label trial, blinded outcome; 14 centres in Spain.
data.limitations: Higher mortality with IA alteplase; modest sample.
data.practiceImpact: IA alteplase after successful EVT improved excellent outcomes and reperfusion — but a higher mortality signal warrants caution before adoption.

data.fullName: Endovascular Treatment for Medium or Distal Vessel Occlusion Stroke — 12-Month Outcomes

population.timeWindow: ≤6 h, or 6-24 h with salvageable tissue

data.imagingCriteria: Salvageable tissue required for 6-24 h window
data.applicabilityNotes: 12-month results consistent with 90-day results; mild-to-moderate MeVO.
data.limitations: Open-label; benefit not excluded in subgroups.
data.practiceImpact: No long-term benefit of EVT in mild-to-moderate medium/distal vessel occlusion — routine thrombectomy not supported in this population.

data.fullName: Endovascular Treatment for Medium Vessel Occlusion Stroke

keyInclusion: Medium vessel occlusion (M2/M3 branches of MCA, A2/A3 branches of ACA, P2/P3 branches of PCA)

safetyFindings.other: Higher 90-day mortality in the EVT group (safety signal)

data.applicabilityNotes: No functional benefit and showed a signal of increased 90-day mortality and sICH.
data.limitations: Open-label trial; ended early for enrollment feasibility.
data.practiceImpact: Does not support routine endovascular thrombectomy for isolated medium vessel occlusion stroke outside of clinical trials.

data.fullName: Direct versus Conventional Transfer to Angiography Suite in Patients with Severe Acute Stroke Treated with Thrombectomy (France)

keyInclusion: Acute severe neurological deficit highly suggestive of LVO (ASND-LVO)

data.applicabilityNotes: Stopped early for safety after interim analysis; 10 comprehensive stroke centres.
data.limitations: Small n (115) limits precision of all estimates.
data.practiceImpact: Bypassing pre-EVT imaging increased sICH without benefit and was halted early — cautions against direct-to-angio in suspected LVO.

data.fullName: Catheter Ablation and Oral Anticoagulation for Secondary Stroke Prevention in Atrial Fibrillation

primaryEndpoint.definition: Composite: recurrent ischaemic stroke, systemic embolism, all-cause death, HF hospitalisation

data.applicabilityNotes: Open-label; 45 sites in Japan; lower-than-anticipated event rate.
data.limitations: Underpowered (low event rate); open-label.
data.practiceImpact: Adding ablation to anticoagulation did not reduce recurrent events in post-stroke AF (underpowered) — does not support routine ablation for secondary prevention.

data.fullName: Increased Risk of Suicide after Stroke: A Population-Based Matched Cohort Study

keyInclusion: 64,719 stroke patients matched 1:1 to Ontario population controls

primaryEndpoint.definition: Suicide (composite of deliberate self-harm or death by suicide)
primaryEndpoint.result: Higher in stroke survivors: 11.1 vs 3.2 per 10,000 person-years

data.applicabilityNotes: Association did not vary by presence of major depression.
data.limitations: Observational — residual confounding; administrative-data ascertainment; association not causation.
data.practiceImpact: Supports long-term (beyond 1 year) suicidality screening in survivors — especially younger and lower-income patients.

data.fullName: Severity-Stratified Hyponatremia Is Associated with Increased Mortality and Complications in Nontraumatic Intracerebral Hemorrhage

secondaryEndpoints.name: Complications
secondaryEndpoints.result: More seizures, cerebral edema, hydrocephalus, EVD, tracheostomy, PEG, DVT, MI

data.applicabilityNotes: Serum sodium as a readily-available risk-stratification marker in acute ICH.
data.limitations: RWE — coding granularity; illness-severity confounding; whether correcting Na changes outcome is unproven.
data.practiceImpact: Serum sodium is a readily-available risk-stratification marker in acute ICH; supports vigilant Na monitoring (causality not established).

data.fullName: Real-World Efficacy and Safety of Tenecteplase versus Alteplase in Acute Ischemic Stroke: A Propensity Score-Matched Analysis
data.applicabilityNotes: Real-world support for tenecteplase as a practical alteplase alternative.
data.limitations: Small single-centre; no point estimate / CI reported; underpowered for sICH; selection bias.
data.practiceImpact: Adds real-world support for tenecteplase as a practical alteplase alternative — consistent with trial-level non-inferiority.

data.fullName: Intravenous Tenecteplase before Thrombectomy in Stroke

keyInclusion: Large-vessel occlusion within 4.5 h of onset
Eligible for IV thrombolysis and for thrombectomy

data.intervention: IV tenecteplase 0.25 mg/kg (max 25 mg) then endovascular thrombectomy
data.applicabilityNotes: Supports giving IV tenecteplase before thrombectomy (rather than bypassing thrombolysis) in thrombolysis-eligible LVO presenting within 4.5 h. Does not address bridging in later windows.
data.limitations: Open-label; single-country (China); generalizability to other populations and EVT-access settings uncertain.
data.practiceImpact: Reinforces bridging IV thrombolysis before thrombectomy for eligible LVO within 4.5 h; do not routinely skip IVT in eligible patients headed to EVT.
data.verificationNotes: PubMed 40396577; NCT04733742. Primary numbers from published abstract, verified 2026-07-06.

data.fullName: Alteplase for Acute Ischemic Stroke at 4.5 to 24 Hours: The HOPE Randomized Clinical Trial
data.imagingCriteria: CT perfusion — salvageable tissue / target mismatch
data.applicabilityNotes: Late-window (4.5-24 h) IV alteplase in perfusion-selected patients not going to thrombectomy. Emerging evidence; not a standard AHA/ASA recommendation. China-only, open-label with blinded endpoint.
data.limitations: Single-country (China); higher sICH; extended-window IVT selection criteria still being defined; do not phrase as routine thrombolysis to 24 h.
data.practiceImpact: Adds positive evidence for perfusion-selected late-window IV thrombolysis in EVT-ineligible patients; remains selective and consult-dependent, not routine.

data.fullName: Alteplase for Posterior Circulation Ischemic Stroke at 4.5 to 24 Hours

keyInclusion: No extensive early hypodensity on CT

data.imagingCriteria: NCCT (no extensive early hypodensity); no perfusion required
data.applicabilityNotes: Late-window (4.5-24 h) IV alteplase for mainly mild posterior-circulation stroke not going to thrombectomy. Emerging evidence; not a standard AHA/ASA recommendation. China-only; mostly low NIHSS.
data.limitations: Single-country (China); mild strokes predominate; benefit in severe posterior-circulation stroke not established.
data.practiceImpact: Supports late-window thrombolysis for selected mild posterior-circulation stroke without perfusion mismatch requirement; remains emerging, not routine.

data.fullName: Minimally Invasive Surgery vs Medical Management Alone for Intracerebral Hemorrhage: The MIND Randomized Clinical Trial
data.intervention: Minimally invasive surgery (Artemis device) + medical management

safetyFindings.other: Enrollment stopped early (n=236) after a contemporaneous positive ICH trial

data.applicabilityNotes: MIS with the Artemis device did NOT improve 180-day outcome or reduce 30-day mortality vs medical management. Contrast with ENRICH (positive for a defined lobar-ICH pathway). Early stop limits power.
data.limitations: Stopped early; underpowered; device- and protocol-specific. A negative result — does not support routine MIS outside a defined pathway.
data.practiceImpact: Tempers enthusiasm for minimally invasive ICH evacuation; MIS benefit appears pathway- and technique-specific (cf. ENRICH), not universal.

data.fullName: Tenecteplase Thrombolysis for Stroke up to 24 Hours After Onset With Perfusion Imaging Selection (CHABLIS-T II)

primaryEndpoint.result: Improved reperfusion without a significant clinical-outcome difference

data.applicabilityNotes: Late-window perfusion-selected TNK; improved reperfusion but no clear clinical benefit. Emerging; not a standard recommendation.
data.limitations: Modest size; surrogate (reperfusion) improved without confirmed clinical benefit.
data.practiceImpact: Adds to the mixed late-window TNK evidence base; reinforces that reperfusion gains have not consistently translated to clinical benefit.
data.verificationNotes: PubMed 39744861. Effect summary from trial report; verified 2026-07-06.

data.fullName: Tenecteplase versus Standard of Care for Minor Ischaemic Stroke with Proven Occlusion (TEMPO-2)

primaryEndpoint.result: No benefit of tenecteplase vs standard of care

data.applicabilityNotes: Thrombolysis did not improve outcomes in minor stroke with occlusion and showed a mortality signal — supports NOT routinely thrombolysing minor (NIHSS 0-5) non-disabling stroke on the basis of occlusion alone.
data.limitations: Applies to minor/non-disabling deficits; does not address disabling deficits with occlusion.
data.practiceImpact: Argues against routine thrombolysis for minor non-disabling stroke solely because an occlusion is present; keep the disabling-deficit gate.

data.fullName: Prevention of Stroke in Intracerebral Haemorrhage Survivors with Atrial Fibrillation

population.timeWindow: Spontaneous ICH survivors with AF, mRS ≤4
keyInclusion: Spontaneous ICH survivor with AF and an anticoagulation indication

primaryEndpoint.definition: Coprimary: first ischaemic stroke (superiority) and first recurrent ICH (non-inferiority), ITT
primaryEndpoint.result: Ischaemic stroke markedly reduced (0.83 vs 8.60 per 100 patient-years); recurrent ICH did NOT meet non-inferiority (5.00 vs 0.82 per 100 patient-years)
primaryEndpoint.pValue: ischaemic p<0.0001; ICH non-inferiority not met

data.applicabilityNotes: Dedicated ICH-survivor RCT: DOAC lowers ischaemic stroke but raises recurrent ICH; net benefit uncertain in a small trial with few events.
data.limitations: Small (n=319); wide CIs; source uses discordant CI conventions (95% for ischaemic HR, 90% for ICH HR).
data.practiceImpact: Objective: anticoagulation after ICH in AF trades a large ischaemic-stroke reduction against a substantial recurrent-ICH increase; individualize.

data.fullName: Edoxaban for Intracranial Hemorrhage Survivors with Atrial Fibrillation

primaryEndpoint.definition: Coprimary: stroke/systemic embolism; ISTH major haemorrhage
primaryEndpoint.result: Full efficacy not yet reported; the lobar-ICH subgroup was stopped by the DSMB for excess recurrent haemorrhagic stroke

safetyFindings.sich: Excess recurrent haemorrhagic stroke in the lobar-ICH (CAA-enriched) subgroup → edoxaban stopped in that subgroup

data.applicabilityNotes: Ongoing; the only reported result to date is a safety signal in lobar ICH. Do not infer net benefit from ENRICH-AF alone.
data.limitations: Primary efficacy pending (2026); lobar-arm-stopped subgroup details partly from secondary reporting.
data.practiceImpact: Objective: interim DSMB stopped edoxaban in lobar ICH for excess rebleeding; the main efficacy comparison is awaited.
data.verificationNotes: Design/NCT (NCT03950076) and lobar-arm-stopped safety signal verified; full primary efficacy result not yet published (expected 2026).

data.fullName: Start or Stop Anticoagulants Randomised Trial

population.timeWindow: Survivors of spontaneous intracranial haemorrhage with AF (CHA₂DS₂-VASc ≥2)
keyInclusion: Spontaneous intracranial haemorrhage survivor with AF

primaryEndpoint.result: Inconclusive: 8/101 (8%) start vs 4/102 (4%) avoid; starting NOT shown non-inferior

safetyFindings.sich: Recurrent ICH numerically higher with anticoagulation (imprecise)

data.applicabilityNotes: Pilot-phase non-inferiority RCT; underpowered and inconclusive for the ICH-survivor anticoagulation question.
data.limitations: Small pilot (n=203); wide CIs; open-label.
data.practiceImpact: Objective: did not establish non-inferiority of starting anticoagulation after ICH; hypothesis-generating.

data.fullName: Apixaban After Anticoagulation-Associated Intracerebral Haemorrhage in Atrial Fibrillation

population.timeWindow: 7-90 d after anticoagulation-associated ICH; AF, CHA₂DS₂-VASc ≥2, mRS ≤4

primaryEndpoint.definition: Composite of non-fatal stroke or vascular death
primaryEndpoint.result: No difference: 26% (12.6%/y) apixaban vs 24% (11.9%/y) avoid; high absolute risk in both arms

safetyFindings.other: Serious adverse events ~57-58% in both arms

data.applicabilityNotes: Phase-2 feasibility RCT to estimate event rates; not powered for efficacy. ~12%/y absolute risk of non-fatal stroke or vascular death regardless of arm.
data.limitations: Small phase-2 (n=101); imprecise estimate.
data.practiceImpact: Objective: no clear difference; both strategies carry high residual vascular risk.

data.fullName: Oral Anticoagulation after Intracranial Haemorrhage in AF: Individual-Patient-Data Meta-analysis

population.timeWindow: AF with prior spontaneous intracranial haemorrhage (pooled RCTs: SoSTART, APACHE-AF, NASPAF-ICH)

safetyFindings.sich: Recurrent bleeding numerically higher with anticoagulation (NS, wide CI)

data.applicabilityNotes: IPD meta-analysis of the completed pre-2025 RCTs. Anticoagulation significantly cut ischaemic events; the composite of any stroke/CV death favoured anticoagulation but was not statistically significant; recurrent bleeding was numerically higher.
data.limitations: Pools small trials (n=412); predates PRESTIGE-AF and ENRICH-AF.
data.practiceImpact: Objective synthesis: net benefit of anticoagulation after ICH in AF remains statistically uncertain; ischaemic reduction is offset by a non-significant bleeding increase.

data.fullName: Decompressive Craniectomy plus Best Medical Treatment vs Best Medical Treatment Alone for Severe Deep ICH
data.intervention: Decompressive craniectomy (no clot evacuation) + best medical treatment (n=96 analysed)

primaryEndpoint.definition: mRS 5-6 (death or very severe disability) at 180 d
primaryEndpoint.result: Favoured surgery numerically but not significant: 44% vs 58%

safetyFindings.other: Severe adverse events 41% (surgery) vs 44% (medical) — no excess with surgery

data.applicabilityNotes: First RCT of decompressive craniectomy (without evacuation) for severe deep ICH — a distinct surgical modality from clot evacuation (ENRICH/MISTIE III).
data.limitations: Modest size (n=201); primary-outcome CI crossed the null.
data.practiceImpact: Objective: numerically fewer very-poor outcomes with craniectomy, but not statistically significant; no safety excess.

data.fullName: Intensive vs Conventional Blood Pressure Lowering after Successful Endovascular Thrombectomy

keyInclusion: LVO stroke with successful reperfusion after EVT

data.applicabilityNotes: Second RCT (with ENCHANTED2/MT) showing harm from intensive BP lowering after successful EVT; supports avoiding SBP <140.
data.limitations: Single-country (South Korea); open-label with blinded endpoints.
data.practiceImpact: Objective: intensive SBP <140 after successful EVT reduced functional independence; reinforces ENCHANTED2/MT.
